heading: Special rules where section 754 election or substantial built-in loss
label: 743 Special rules where section 754 election or substantial built-in loss
notes: References in TextSection 3(a)(1)(A), (c)(1), (7) of the Investment Company Act of 1940, referred to in subsec. (e)(5)(B), is classified to section 80a–3(a)(1)(A), (c)(1), (7) of Title 15, Commerce and Trade.

Amendments2017—Subsec. (d)(1). , amended par. (1) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “For purposes of this section, a partnership has a substantial built-in loss with respect to a transfer of an interest in a partnership if the partnership’s adjusted basis in the partnership property exceeds by more than $250,000 the fair market value of such property.”Pub. children.heading: General rule
children.label: a General rule
children.text: The basis of partnership property shall not be adjusted as the result of a transfer of an interest in a partnership by sale or exchange or on the death of a partner unless the election provided by section 754 (relating to optional adjustment to basis of partnership property) is in effect with respect to such partnership or unless the partnership has a substantial built-in loss immediately after such transfer.

children.heading: Adjustment to basis of partnership property
children.label: b Adjustment to basis of partnership property

children.text: increase the adjusted basis of the partnership property by the excess of the basis to the transferee partner of his interest in the partnership over his proportionate share of the adjusted basis of the partnership property, or

decrease the adjusted basis of the partnership property by the excess of the transferee partner’s proportionate share of the adjusted basis of the partnership property over the basis of his interest in the partnership.

children.heading: Allocation of basis
children.label: c Allocation of basis
children.text: The allocation of basis among partnership properties where subsection (b) is applicable shall be made in accordance with the rules provided in section 755.

children.label: A
children.text: the partnership’s adjusted basis in the partnership property exceeds by more than $250,000 the fair market value of such property, or

children.label: B
children.text: the transferee partner would be allocated a loss of more than $250,000 if the partnership assets were sold for cash equal to their fair market value immediately after such transfer.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 2 Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be appropriate to carry out the purposes of paragraph (1) and section 734(d), including regulations aggregating related partnerships and disregarding property acquired by the partnership in an attempt to avoid such purposes.

children.heading: Alternative rules for electing investment partnerships
children.label: e Alternative rules for electing investment partnerships

children.heading: No adjustment of partnership basis
children.label: 1 No adjustment of partnership basis
children.text: For purposes of this section, an electing investment partnership shall not be treated as having a substantial built-in loss with respect to any transfer occurring while the election under paragraph (6)(A) is in effect.

children.heading: Loss deferral for transferee partner
children.label: 2 Loss deferral for transferee partner
children.text: In the case of a transfer of an interest in an electing investment partnership, the transferee partner’s distributive share of losses (without regard to gains) from the sale or exchange of partnership property shall not be allowed except to the extent that it is established that such losses exceed the loss (if any) recognized by the transferor (or any prior transferor to the extent not fully offset by a prior disallowance under this paragraph) on the transfer of the partnership interest.

children.heading: No reduction in partnership basis
children.label: 3 No reduction in partnership basis
children.text: Losses disallowed under paragraph (2) shall not decrease the transferee partner’s basis in the partnership interest.

children.heading: Certain basis reductions treated as losses
children.label: 4 Certain basis reductions treated as losses
children.text: In the case of a transferee partner whose basis in property distributed by the partnership is reduced under section 732(a)(2), the amount of the loss recognized by the transferor on the transfer of the partnership interest which is taken into account under paragraph (2) shall be reduced by the amount of such basis reduction.

children.label: A
children.text: the partnership makes an election to have this subsection apply,

children.label: B
children.text: the partnership would be an investment company under section 3(a)(1)(A) of the Investment Company Act of 1940 but for an exemption under paragraph (1) or (7) of section 3(c) of such Act,

children.label: C
children.text: such partnership has never been engaged in a trade or business,

children.label: D
children.text: substantially all of the assets of such partnership are held for investment,

children.label: E
children.text: at least 95 percent of the assets contributed to such partnership consist of money,

children.label: F
children.text: no assets contributed to such partnership had an adjusted basis in excess of fair market value at the time of contribution,

children.label: G
children.text: all partnership interests of such partnership are issued by such partnership pursuant to a private offering before the date which is 24 months after the date of the first capital contribution to such partnership,

children.label: H
children.text: the partnership agreement of such partnership has substantive restrictions on each partner’s ability to cause a redemption of the partner’s interest, and

children.label: I
children.text: the partnership agreement of such partnership provides for a term that is not in excess of 15 years.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 6 Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be appropriate to carry out the purposes of this subsection, including regulations for applying this subsection to tiered partnerships.

children.heading: No adjustment of partnership basis
children.label: 1 No adjustment of partnership basis
children.text: For purposes of this section, a securitization partnership shall not be treated as having a substantial built-in loss with respect to any transfer.

children.heading: Securitization partnership
children.label: 2 Securitization partnership
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), the term “securitization partnership” means any partnership the sole business activity of which is to issue securities which provide for a fixed principal (or similar) amount and which are primarily serviced by the cash flows of a discrete pool (either fixed or revolving) of receivables or other financial assets that by their terms convert into cash in a finite period, but only if the sponsor of the pool reasonably believes that the receivables and other financial assets comprising the pool are not acquired so as to be disposed of.
